summary: Excellent soundboard capture of a classic ’71 show with standout Sugar Magnolia wah‑wah and an unusual Wharf Rat ending.
review: The recording delivers crystal‑clear, classic 1971 soundboard quality where Jerry’s guitar shines—especially his wah‑wah on Sugar Magnolia. Fans also note the psychedelic vibe of the performance and the rare, clean finish to Wharf Rat, making this a must‑listen for Deadheads who enjoy that era.

ai_rating.rationale: All three reviews are highly favorable (5, 5, 4 stars), praising both the musical performance and the pristine soundboard recording; no negatives were reported.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, clear mix with every guitar line audible; classic ’71 tone praised. No drop‑outs or distortion mentioned, only suggestions to tweak EQ for optimal playback.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The set flows as a psychedelic ’71 adventure, highlighted by a unique, clean ending to Wharf Rat rather than the usual fade‑out.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s guitar is described as amazing, with every line clear and a notable wah‑wah pedal use on Sugar Magnolia.
